snippets.title: CLI: profile a whole script
snippets.code:
# Run and print sorted top-20:
python -m cProfile -s cumulative script.py | head -40

# Save to disk for later analysis / visualization:
python -m cProfile -o profile.out script.py arg1 arg2

# Inspect later:
python -c "import pstats; pstats.Stats('profile.out').strip_dirs().sort_stats('cumulative').print_stats(30)"

# Filter by file/function name pattern (regex):
python -c "import pstats; pstats.Stats('profile.out').sort_stats('tottime').print_stats('retrieval')"
snippets.explanation: Saving to a file and analysing later (`pstats`) is the most flexible workflow — sort multiple ways, filter to a subsystem.

snippets.title: API: profile a region
snippets.code:
import cProfile, pstats

def expensive_thing():
    ...

with cProfile.Profile() as pr:
    expensive_thing()

stats = pstats.Stats(pr).strip_dirs()
stats.sort_stats('cumulative').print_stats(20)
# Or sort by self-time to find the actual work:
stats.sort_stats('tottime').print_stats(20)
snippets.explanation: Profile *just the region you care about* instead of the whole program — clearer signal, smaller output.

snippets.title: Reusable `@profiled` decorator
snippets.explanation: Drop the decorator on the function you're investigating; remove it when done. Far less noisy than profiling the whole script.

snippets.title: Visualize with snakeviz
snippets.code:
pip install snakeviz

# Generate the profile:
python -m cProfile -o profile.out script.py

# Open in browser (icicle chart):
snakeviz profile.out
snippets.explanation: An icicle chart shows time proportionally. Clicking dives into a frame; you see at a glance where the time pools.

snippets.title: Compare before/after
snippets.code:
# Baseline:
python -m cProfile -o before.out script.py

# After your optimization:
python -m cProfile -o after.out script.py

# Quick diff with pstats:
python -c "
import pstats
print('=== BEFORE ==='); pstats.Stats('before.out').strip_dirs().sort_stats('cumulative').print_stats(15)
print('=== AFTER ===');  pstats.Stats('after.out' ).strip_dirs().sort_stats('cumulative').print_stats(15)
"
snippets.explanation: Apples-to-apples comparison. Without before/after numbers, every optimisation is a hypothesis.
